metadata.sources: HomeAdvisor, Angi, BLS SOC 47-2152, contractor surveys

commonUpsells.notes: Useful for diagnosing hidden issues. Not needed for simple clogs.
Only if water quality test shows issues.
Required by code in some areas with closed water systems.
Pressure reducing valve. Only if water pressure exceeds 80 PSI.

value.notes: Big-box builder-grade gas/electric tanks. 6-year warranty typical. Parts availability thin after year 8.

mid.notes: Mainstream contractor-grade. 6-9 year tank warranty. Rheem Performance Plus / A.O. Smith ProLine sit here.

premium.notes: Contractor-exclusive (Bradford White is not sold to DIY). 10-year warranty, heavier steel, better anodes.

value.notes: Entry tankless. Adequate for 1-2 bath homes in warm climates.

mid.notes: Recirculation-ready condensing tankless. Best sweet spot for 2-3 bath homes.

premium.notes: Ultra-high-efficiency condensing, stainless HX, 15-year HX warranty.

value.notes: Entry HPWH. Qualifies for IRA 25C credit (30% up to $2,000) if UEF >= 2.2.

mid.notes: Most common residential HPWH. UEF 3.55-4.0. IRA 25C eligible.

premium.notes: Commercial-grade HPWH, 10-year tank warranty, 120V plug-in options. IRA 25C eligible.

value.notes: PEX-B crimp or push-fit. Good for small repairs; not ideal for whole-home where expansion PEX-A shines.

mid.notes: Home-run ManaBloc manifold systems. Balances cost and reliability.

premium.notes: Expansion PEX-A with cold-expansion fittings. 25-year warranty, highest burst resistance, no dezincification risk.

value.notes: Thinnest wall copper allowed for residential. Not recommended in aggressive water markets.

mid.notes: Standard residential copper. 50+ year life in most water chemistries.

premium.notes: Heaviest wall, required for underground service lines in many jurisdictions.

iraCredit.section: 25C Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit (Heat Pump Water Heater)
iraCredit.expirationNote: The 25C federal tax credit expired Dec 31 2025. HPWH installs on or after Jan 1 2026 do NOT qualify. State utility rebates and IRA HEAR (income-qualified) programs may still apply.

utilityRebates.notes: HPWH rebate stacks on IRA 25C credit.
Through participating contractors. Low/moderate income adders available.
Gas-to-electric fuel-switch bonus in Bay Area.

AL.board: Alabama Plumbers and Gas Fitters Examining Board
AL.notes: Master/Journeyman license required for all plumbing work.

AK.board: AK Dept of Labor Mechanical Inspection

AR.board: AR Dept of Health Plumbing Board

CA.notes: C-36 license; $25,000 bond; work over $500 requires license.

FL.board: FL DBPR Construction Industry Licensing Board
FL.notes: Certified Plumbing Contractor (CFC) or Registered local license.

GA.board: GA State Construction Industry Licensing Board

ID.board: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses

IL.board: IL Dept of Public Health Plumbing Program

IA.board: Iowa Plumbing and Mechanical Systems Board

KS.notes: No statewide license; licensed locally (Wichita, KCK, Topeka).

LA.notes: Master plumber license; work over $10k also needs LSLBC.

MA.board: MA Board of State Examiners of Plumbers and Gas Fitters
MA.notes: Master plumber required. Strict enforcement; HIC registration also required.

MN.board: MN Dept of Labor and Industry

MS.notes: Commercial >$50k; residential license for HVAC/plumb over $10k.

MO.notes: Statewide registration only; city licenses in STL, KC, Springfield.

NE.notes: Licensed at city level (Omaha, Lincoln); state registers contractors.

NJ.board: NJ State Board of Master Plumbers

NY.notes: Licensed locally — NYC, Buffalo, Syracuse, Nassau, Suffolk each have their own plumbing license.

NC.board: NC State Board of Examiners of Plumbing Heating and Fire Sprinkler Contractors

OH.notes: Plumbing Contractor statewide for commercial; residential varies by municipality.

PA.notes: No statewide plumbing license. HICPA registration required for residential remodel. Local license (Philly, Pittsburgh).

RI.board: RI Contractors Registration and Licensing Board

TX.board: Texas State Board of Plumbing Examiners (TSBPE)

WA.notes: Pl 01 Journey Plumber / Plumbing Contractor registration.

WI.board: WI Dept of Safety and Professional Services

WY.notes: No statewide plumbing license; city-level (Cheyenne, Casper).

redFlagPatterns.name: no_permit_water_heater
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Water heater replacement requires a permit and final inspection in nearly every U.S. jurisdiction. Unpermitted installs fail home-sale inspections, void manufacturer warranties, and can void homeowners insurance after a flood or gas leak. Also violates IRC P2801 and IFGC code.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must read 'contractor pulls and pays plumbing/mechanical permit and coordinates final inspection.'

redFlagPatterns.name: no_permit_repipe_sewer
redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(repipe|re-pipe|whole house repipe|sewer line replace|main line replace|underground service line|water main)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Whole-house repipe, sewer line replacement, and water service line work all require permits and inspections. Unpermitted underground work is nearly impossible to fix at resale.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require permit + inspection line item. Ask for the permit number once pulled.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_material_spec_repipe
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Repipe quote without specifying PEX-A vs PEX-B vs copper Type L/M is unenforceable. PEX-A (expansion) has a 25-year warranty; PEX-B crimp can dezincify in aggressive water. Type M copper is the thinnest allowed and not ideal. Price difference is 2-3x between bottom and top spec.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require: manufacturer + type (e.g., 'Uponor AquaPEX PEX-A, 1/2" and 3/4"') and total linear feet.

redFlagPatterns.name: galvanized_or_cast_iron_patch
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Galvanized supply lines (pre-1960) and cast iron drain stacks fail systemically — patching one section just pushes the next leak downstream. Insurance companies increasingly deny water-damage claims on homes with known galvanized/cast iron still in service.
redFlagPatterns.action: Get a second opinion on full replacement of the affected run. Patching is rarely worth the cost.

redFlagPatterns.name: today_only_urgency
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Urgency pressure on a plumbing quote is a tell. Legitimate plumbing quotes are valid 7-30 days; water heater and fixture prices do not change hourly. Emergency leak repairs are the rare exception.
redFlagPatterns.action: Refuse to sign in the same visit unless water is actively flowing. Get quote validity in writing.

redFlagPatterns.name: phone_only_major_quote
redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(phone estimate|over the phone|sight unseen|without (seeing|inspecting)|quoted by phone)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Repipe, sewer line, water heater, and main line work cannot be honestly quoted by phone. Phone quotes are low-ball bait; the real price arrives mid-job as 'change orders.'
redFlagPatterns.action: Require on-site inspection before signing anything. Reputable plumbers will not quote major work blind.

redFlagPatterns.name: excessive_deposit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Deposit over 33% on plumbing work. Industry standard is 10-33% at signing for material orders, balance on substantial completion. Several states cap home improvement deposits (MD/ME at 33%, CA at 10% or $1,000 whichever less).
redFlagPatterns.action: Cap deposit at one-third. Remainder on substantial completion. Never pay in full up front.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_license_number
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No state plumbing license number on the quote. Plumbing is licensed statewide in 45+ states. Unlicensed plumbing work voids insurance claims and can't be permitted or inspected. AARP lists unlicensed home services as a top-5 elder fraud category.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify the state plumbing license number before any deposit. Most state boards have free online lookup.

redFlagPatterns.name: excessive_diagnostic_fee
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Diagnostic or service call fee over $200. Reasonable range is $49-$150 ($200-$250 for after-hours). Anything higher is usually an upsell anchor — the plumber expects to 'waive' it if you sign the work order.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask whether the diagnostic fee is credited toward work performed. If not, get a second opinion before authorizing work.

redFlagPatterns.name: emergency_markup_undisclosed
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Emergency/after-hours language without a clearly disclosed surcharge amount or multiplier. Emergency plumbing markups typically run 1.5x-2.5x the normal rate; hiding them leads to invoice shock.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require the emergency multiplier or flat surcharge in writing BEFORE work begins.

redFlagPatterns.name: vague_scope_language
redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(standard install(ation)?|per (industry|company) standard|as discussed|per conversation|as needed|to be determined|tbd)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Verbal promises without written scope detail are unenforceable. 'Per standard installation' means nothing when the $600 change order arrives.
redFlagPatterns.action: Every promise goes on the contract or walk away.

redFlagPatterns.name: missing_warranty
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No warranty on parts or labor stated. Minimum acceptable is 1 year labor + manufacturer parts warranty. Reputable plumbers offer 2-5 year labor on major work like repipe and water heater installs.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require minimum 1-year labor warranty in writing, plus manufacturer parts warranty.

redFlagPatterns.name: expansion_tank_missing_closed_system
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Water heater replacement on a closed system (PRV or backflow preventer present) requires a thermal expansion tank by code (IPC/UPC). Missing it will fail inspection and can rupture the tank under thermal expansion.
redFlagPatterns.action: Confirm whether home is on a closed system. If yes, require expansion tank line item ($200-$400 installed).

redFlagPatterns.name: sewer_replace_no_scope
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Sewer line replacement quote without trenchless vs open-trench method, linear footage, and camera inspection results. Price difference is 2-4x between the two methods, and quotes without footage hide change-order risk.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require: method (trenchless pipe burst, CIPP lining, or open trench), linear footage, depth, camera inspection video, and permit.

redFlagPatterns.name: repipe_no_drywall_repair
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Whole-house repipe requires opening 20-80 access holes in drywall. If drywall patch/texture/paint is not in the scope, the homeowner is stuck finding a separate drywall contractor for $1,500-$5,000 of additional work.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must specify whether access-hole drywall repair is included, and to what finish level (patched only, textured, textured + painted).

redFlagPatterns.name: no_line_item_breakdown
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Single 'all-in' price with no parts/labor split. Prevents comparison shopping and hides markups on fixtures. Industry standard on any job over $500 is a line-item scope.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require parts list with quantities + labor hours + hourly rate as separate line items.

redFlagPatterns.name: homeowner_pulls_permit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Quote pushes permit responsibility to homeowner. This is a red flag that the plumber may not be licensed, or wants to distance themselves from liability if work fails inspection. Homeowners who pull permits become the legally responsible 'contractor.'
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must say 'contractor pulls and pays all required permits.' If plumber refuses, they may not be licensed — verify.

redFlagPatterns.name: cash_only_no_receipt
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Cash-only with no written invoice. Classic unlicensed/unbonded operator signature. You have no recourse for warranty, no proof for insurance, and no documentation for home sale.
redFlagPatterns.action: Walk away. A legitimate plumber will accept check or card and issue a written invoice.

redFlagPatterns.name: po_box_or_out_of_state
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Address is a PO box with no local physical address. Warranty service becomes impossible and disputes are unenforceable. Common with out-of-state storm-chaser operators.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify a physical local address, active state plumbing license, and local Google/BBB reviews before deposit.

recommendedScopeItems: Exact job description with linear footage (repipe/sewer) or unit count (fixtures)
Pipe material brand and type (e.g., Uponor AquaPEX PEX-A Type L copper)
Water heater brand, model number, capacity, fuel type, and UEF
Parts list with quantities, itemized separately from labor
Labor rate, estimated hours, and labor total
Contractor pulls and pays all required permits
Permit and final inspection included as line items
Expansion tank included if home is on a closed system
Drywall access-hole repair scope (patched, textured, painted)
Haul-away and disposal of old equipment
State plumbing license number on the quote
Deposit capped at 33%, balance on substantial completion
Manufacturer QM PIN provided for heat pump water heater 25C claim
Diagnostic/service fee disclosed and creditable toward work
questionsToAsk: What is your state plumbing license number, and can I verify it online?
Will you pull and pay for the permit, or am I expected to?
For repipe: what is the exact pipe brand and type — PEX-A, PEX-B, Type L copper?
For water heater: what brand, model, and UEF? Is it ENERGY STAR?
Is this quote a firm bid or time-and-materials? What is the not-to-exceed?
How long is this quote valid?
What is your labor rate, and how many hours are estimated for this job?
Is my house on a closed water system? If yes, is an expansion tank included?
For repipe: how many drywall access holes, and is patch/texture/paint included?
For sewer: trenchless or open trench? How many linear feet? Can I see the camera video?
What warranty do you offer on parts, and what on labor?
What is the deposit, and when is the balance due?
Do you carry general liability insurance and workers comp? Can I see certificates?
Is the diagnostic/service fee credited toward work performed?
